notes: Assertions on structural boilerplate (vcShouldKeep, required fields, docker pinning, no forbidden fields, register_module_line) pass 100% in BOTH configurations — model knows these conventions without the skill. These assertions are non-discriminating for measuring skill value.

Skill value is concentrated in eval 2 (alert-context-ip-enrichment): without the skill, the model omits CustomFields flattening and uses demisto.executeCommand() instead of execute_command(). Both are subtle XSIAM-specific patterns the skill correctly teaches.

Naming convention is a qualitative difference not captured in assertions: with_skill uses kebab-case ('enrich-alert-ips'), without_skill uses PascalCase ('EnrichAlertIPs'). This aligns with the plugin's custom automation naming convention.

Evals 1 and 3 show no measurable difference between skill and baseline — both produce correct, importable YAML. The skill adds ~7s and ~2100 tokens of overhead on these tasks without correctness benefit.

Eval 2 without_skill demonstrates a real bug: omitting CustomFields flattening means src_ip/dest_ip in CustomFields would silently return empty strings, causing the script to silently no-op on many real XSIAM alerts.
